brief.objective: explain the PRIME instructional materials correlation process
keyMessages: WIDA PRIME lists instructional materials that have gone through the PRIME process and received a PRIME seal.
PRIME 2020 includes materials aligned to the current WIDA English Language Development Standards Framework, 2020 Edition.
WIDA PRIME information helps educators consider how particular materials fit in their program.
proofPoints: The WIDA PRIME site lists reviewed PRIME v1 and v2 correlations and PRIME 2020 alignments.
The WIDA PRIME site says PRIME 2020 includes materials aligned to the current WIDA English Language Development Standards Framework, 2020 Edition.
The WIDA PRIME site says WIDA does not endorse instructional materials and PRIME is not an evaluative tool that judges effectiveness.
brief.layoutRecommendation: Use a single-column HTML email announcement with a logo header, concise message, one CTA button, and inline styles.

headlineOptions: Explore WIDA PRIME correlations and alignments
Clear information for instructional materials decisions
subheadOptions: WIDA PRIME lists instructional materials that have gone through the PRIME process and received a PRIME seal.
WIDA PRIME lists instructional materials that have gone through the PRIME process and received a PRIME seal.
WIDA PRIME lists instructional materials that have gone through the PRIME process and received a PRIME seal.
copy.body: WIDA PRIME lists instructional materials that have gone through the PRIME process and received a PRIME seal. For publishers, this draft focuses on PRIME 2020, PRIME V1/V2, Spanish Language Development correlations, and publisher workflow.
bullets: WIDA PRIME lists instructional materials that have gone through the PRIME process and received a PRIME seal.
PRIME 2020 includes materials aligned to the current WIDA English Language Development Standards Framework, 2020 Edition.
WIDA PRIME information helps educators consider how particular materials fit in their program.
The WIDA PRIME site lists reviewed PRIME v1 and v2 correlations and PRIME 2020 alignments.
The WIDA PRIME site says PRIME 2020 includes materials aligned to the current WIDA English Language Development Standards Framework, 2020 Edition.

fittedCopy.headline: Explore WIDA PRIME correlations and alignments
fittedCopy.deck: WIDA PRIME lists instructional materials that have gone through the PRIME process and received a PRIME seal.
proofPoints: PRIME 2020 includes materials aligned to the current WIDA English Language Development Standards Framework.
WIDA PRIME information helps educators consider how particular materials fit in their program.

compositionTemplate.typographyScale: email-safe headline with concise body text
compositionTemplate.artPlateTreatment: wide hero art strip with controlled crop
compositionTemplate.copyPlacement: email body stack with 2 proof rows

designRecipe.hierarchy: compact email hierarchy with official logo header, a wide visual hook, then exact headline, deck, proof rows, and CTA in the body
designRecipe.artDirection: wide polished WIDA PRIME hero strip for publishers, using unbranded instructional materials, subtle standards-alignment pathways, and calm blue/teal/green academic color cues to suggest correlations, review, and trusted fit
designRecipe.placeholderStrategy: use subject placement, soft depth, and clean crop-safe negative space to support the email body below; include no readable text, seals, logos, buttons, cards, or UI modules
designRecipe.appComposition: the app renders the official logo header, crops the top-band art, and sets all real headline, body copy, proof rows, CTA, and export in email-safe markup
promptDirectives: Design a wide email hero concept, not a full newsletter or page layout.
Make the alignment/correlation process recognizable for educational publishers at 680px width.
Use unbranded materials and abstract standards-grid/pathway motifs with no readable text.
Avoid fake logos, seals, buttons, contact bars, placeholder cards, or UI modules.

compositionScore.question: Would I send this to the WCEPS team without apology?
warnings: WIDA PRIME prompt is missing avoid guardrail(s): fake title.

layoutQa.question: Would I send this layout to the WCEPS team without apology?
issues: Proof copy is too tall for the selected template.

copyQualityQa.question: Would I send this copy to the WCEPS team without apology?
warnings: Generated copy repeats the same source idea across body, subhead, or bullets before copy-fit.

failureModes.message: Generated copy repeats the same source idea across body, subhead, or bullets before copy-fit.

visualQa.question: Would I send this visual system to the WCEPS team without apology?
warnings: WIDA PRIME visual prompt is missing avoid guardrail(s): fake title.

failureModes.message: WIDA PRIME visual prompt is missing avoid guardrail(s): fake title.

renderQa.question: Would I send this rendered artifact to the WCEPS team without apology?
issues: Layout QA must be resolved before export: Proof copy is too tall for the selected template.

failureModes.message: Layout QA must be resolved before export: Proof copy is too tall for the selected template.

modelQa.question: Would a senior WCEPS reviewer send this artifact without apology?
issues: Layout QA reports proof copy overflow for the selected email template and export should not proceed until copy or layout is adjusted.
warnings: Support copy shifts from publisher workflow to how educators use PRIME information, weakening relevance for the requested publisher audience.
The final fitted copy retains repetitive PRIME phrasing and passive wording, making the announcement feel less polished.
Final copy does not use several relevant WIDA PRIME specifics such as PRIME V1/V2 or Spanish Language Development correlations.

safeZones: Create a wide art field that crops cleanly at email width.
Keep the canvas simple enough for a narrow email module.
Avoid tiny details that disappear at email width.
exactTextPriority: Explore WIDA PRIME correlations and alignments
WIDA PRIME lists instructional materials that have gone through the PRIME process and received a PRIME
PRIME 2020 includes materials aligned to the current WIDA English Language Development Standards
WIDA PRIME information helps educators consider how particular materials fit in their program.
appOwnedElements: Official WIDA PRIME logo from approved asset.
Deterministic grid, typography, headline, audience, and CTA.
Exact support copy and proof hierarchy.
Final export wrapper, source evidence, and review state.
imageGenOwnedElements: Atmosphere, photography or illustration treatment, color, texture, and crop rhythm.
No document layout, fake cards, panels, contact bars, charts, or text.

imageResults.revisedPrompt: Create a 1200x700 email-safe full-bleed campaign art plate for a polished publisher-facing WIDA PRIME announcement, but do not include any logos, wordmarks, initials, seals, badges, CTAs, headlines, labels, or readable text. Wide hero-strip design comp, intended to be cropped as the top band of an email with clean negative space supporting app-rendered body content below. Authentic editorial photography / refined abstract-illustrative hybrid: premium education-sector atmosphere showing unbranded K-12 content-area instructional materials, teacher-edition style binders, student workbook spreads, grade-band curriculum packets, sample science/math/language arts/social studies pages with all text fully illegible/abstracted, alignment review notes without labels, and a publisher/correlator workflow table implied only through shapes and materials. Make the standards-alignment/correlation process recognizable through subtle abstract pathways and grid overlays, calm layered depth, precise organized review setting, material-specific and trustworthy. Palette should feel aligned with deep blue #0057A8, teal #00A6A6, green #6CC24A, and amber #F6A800 as restrained accents, with clean white/neutral workspace and premium lighting. Composition: visual hook concentrated in the top band, balanced left-to-right rhythm, crop-safe negative space, minimal density, no full webpage/newsletter layout, no panels/cards/mock UI/blank boxes/fake charts. Avoid random novels, trade books, library stacks, fake publisher brands, fake PRIME seals, endorsement imagery, posed workshop/training-room stock, close-up faces, detailed hands, chevrons, arrows, mountain-like marks, logo-like patterns, readable letters/numbers/URLs/phone/citations/lorem ipsum. No implication of endorsement or effectiveness evaluation; only standards alignment/correlation workflow atmosphere. NY media/ad-agency quality, intentional crop, sophisticated depth, email header art only.

request.goal: explain the PRIME instructional materials correlation process
request.topic: PRIME 2020, PRIME V1/V2, Spanish Language Development correlations, and publisher workflow
request.notes: Do not imply WIDA endorses materials or that PRIME evaluates effectiveness.

sourceEvidence.excerpt: WIDA is pleased to list instructional materials that have gone through the PRIME process and received a PRIME seal.
sourceEvidence.label: Instructional materials listing

sourceEvidence.excerpt: Users may filter for correlations previously correlated with PRIME V1 and PRIME V2, or aligned with PRIME 2020.
sourceEvidence.label: PRIME versions

sourceEvidence.excerpt: PRIME 2020 includes materials that are aligned to the current WIDA English Language Development Standards Framework, 2020 Edition.
sourceEvidence.label: PRIME 2020

sourceEvidence.excerpt: WIDA does not endorse any set of instructional materials. WIDA PRIME is not an evaluative tool that judges the effectiveness of published materials.
sourceEvidence.label: Endorsement guardrail
